research.title: Shared CLI core across monorepo packages — commonalities, layering, and scope
research.question: What are the underlying commonalities any monorepo package CLI needs (pi-context-cli today; future pi-workflows / jit-agent-dispatch CLIs) so we do not duplicate code — a single source for what is best single-source-editable, with each package adding its particulars — from a minimalist best-practices perspective?
research.method: Code-inspection via parallel Explore agents: mapped pi-context-cli (bin.ts/cli.ts) generic-vs-package-specific machinery, and the op/tool registration shapes + execution models of pi-workflows, pi-agent-dispatch, pi-behavior-monitors, pi-jit-agents.
scope: src/ tool registration of pi-workflows / pi-agent-dispatch / pi-behavior-monitors / pi-jit-agents
research.findings_summary:
The duplication risk for monorepo package CLIs is real but narrower than "share OpDefinition."

Corrective finding: OpDefinition is pi-context's INTERNAL wrapper (ops-registry.ts), not a shared contract — registerAll wraps each OpDefinition into pi.registerTool. The contract already single-sourced in-pi is pi-mono's pi.registerTool / AgentToolResult, which all four extensions use identically; there is no in-pi duplication to remove. The risk is entirely in the CLI layer.

What is genuinely common (all generic in pi-context-cli today): argv->params coercion driven purely by the op's typebox parameters schema (--field, @file, scalar/enum/JSON, required-checking); global flags --cwd/--json/--yes/--help; the auth-DECISION logic (authGated + --yes + TTY); help generation; exit codes; and the output layer — OpResult (string|{json}|{read}), renderOpResultText, boundedJsonOutput, and the 50KB cap enforced at the output boundary. That cap was bug-prone this arc (FGAP-013/015: the {json}-channel bypass + the read-.data leak); single-sourcing it stops that class recurring per package. Package-specific is small: the ops registry, a buildContext callback (pi-context's DispatchContext + resolveIdentity + WriterIdentity), the writer-kind vocabulary, and which ops are authGated.

Minimalist shape: one small cli-core package generic over a context type Ctx and a minimal CliOp shape ({name, description, parameters: TSchema, surface, authGated?, run(cwd, params, ctx)}); each package bin ~= runCli({programName, ops, buildContext}). Layering nuance (load-bearing): OpResult/render/cap are used by BOTH the CLI and pi-context's in-pi registerAll (tool text), so they belong in a base both depend on — a tiny op-io package — NOT inside the CLI package (that would invert the extension->CLI dependency). op-io is also where the cap-at-boundary invariant becomes structurally un-bypassable for any surface.

Honest scope limit: a reflection CLI serves only request->response ops. pi-context fits today. pi-workflows fits PARTIALLY (simple ops reflect; workflow-execute/resume are long-running subprocess + checkpoint state + TUI — bespoke, not reflection). pi-agent-dispatch does NOT fit as-is (gating is a pi-runtime pi.on('tool_call') handler). pi-behavior-monitors does NOT fit (tools read runtime state mutated by agent_end handlers). pi-jit-agents is a pure library (no tools, no CLI). So cli-core is the single source for the request->response CLI projection — pi-context now, the request->response subset of pi-workflows later — NOT a uniform four-package CLI. Consistent with the per-package-CLI decision (design-ledger 1c).

Onboarding seam: a package provides an adapter tools->CliOp[]; pi-context's OpDefinition already conforms; pi-workflows wraps its request->response tools' execute->run. The cli-core/op-io split is a PROPOSAL — six open questions (op-io boundary, Ctx-genericity vs a shared identity model, the workflows long-running split, an auth-policy hook, generalizing scripts/parity-check.ts to any cli-core adopter, package count) are recorded in the findings_document; pre-implementation it goes through the canonical pipeline.

dependencies: @earendil-works/pi-coding-agent — pi.registerTool / AgentToolResult is the actual shared in-pi tool contract used identically by all four extensions

stale_conditions: pi-workflows / pi-agent-dispatch / pi-behavior-monitors change their tool registration shape away from the pi-mono pi.registerTool interface.
OpResult / renderOpResultText / boundedJsonOutput / the 50KB boundary cap relocate or change semantics (currently in packages/pi-context/src/ops-registry.ts + read-element.ts).
pi-context-cli's reflection machinery (parseOpArgs / authDecision / the envelope emission) is refactored such that the generic-vs-specific seam shifts.
The per-package-CLI decision (design-ledger 1c) is revisited toward a single meta-CLI.

citations.label: pi-context-cli generic reflection engine (parse/coerce/flags/auth/envelope)
OpDefinition contract — pi-context-internal, not shared
output cap machinery: OpResult / renderOpResultText / boundedJsonOutput / 50KB boundary cap
DispatchContext / WriterIdentity — package-specific run-context
pi-workflows direct pi.registerTool registration + long-running executors (divergent model)
pi-jit-agents is a library, not an extension (no CLI surface)
per-package-CLI decision (1c)
full design doc (deep read)

research.title: Substrate portability — export/import bundles vs an external/global substrate dir
research.question: How can a pi-context substrate be made portable via a `pi-context-cli export / import` command (e.g. encode a project-management model once and import it into other projects so it 'just works'), and/or via a switchable-to substrate that lives at an external `path/to/.substrate-dir` global location — which is feasible, what already exists, and what must be built?
research.method: Code-inspection via three parallel read-only Explore agents over packages/pi-context/src, packages/pi-context-cli/src, packages/pi-workflows/src, and scripts/ at branch context-jit-spec-v2 (post-v0.30.0, HEAD be16688). Agents mapped: (a) the existing model-install/catalog machinery (init/accept-all/install + samples catalog), (b) substrate-dir resolution + switching + external-path feasibility + content-identity, (c) the CLI op-registry reflection surface + the parity gate + the pi-workflows workflow-discovery prior art.
scope: pi-project-workflows monorepo, branch context-jit-spec-v2, post-v0.30.0 (HEAD be16688)
packages/pi-context/src/{context.ts, context-dir.ts, index.ts, block-api.ts, ops-registry.ts, content-hash.ts, object-store.ts, samples-catalog.ts}
research.findings_summary:
The prompt holds TWO non-interchangeable mechanisms. Fork 1 — export/import (file bundle): transport a COPY; materialize an independent instance in the target project. Fork 2 — external/global path/to/.substrate-dir: share ONE live substrate across projects. The user's PM-import example is Fork 1, and specifically a MODEL export (vocabulary), not instance content.

Second load-bearing split: a substrate = MODEL (portable: config.json registries — block_kinds, relation_types, hierarchy, lenses, invariants, layers, status_buckets, display_strings, naming, installed_schemas/blocks, tool_operations; plus schemas/*.schema.json) vs INSTANCE (not portable: each block's items, relations.json, substrate_id, root). ConfigBlock at context.ts:39; REGISTRY_DESCRIPTORS at :942.

Fork 1 — import is ALREADY HALF-BUILT, just hardcoded to the packaged catalog. /context init (initProject, index.ts:250) + /context accept-all (adoptConception, context.ts:873, reads samples/conception.json, never-clobber guard :882) + /context install (installContext, index.ts:334, copies installed_schemas/blocks from samples/ with skip-if-exists unless --update :387) IS 'import from a bundle'. Directionality is strictly one-way today (grep finds no export producer). The seam is two changes: (a) parameterize the source — adoptConception(cwd,{conceptionPath?}) + installContext(cwd,{schemasRoot?,blocksRoot?}), default packaged (unchanged), override to a bundle dir; then /context import <bundle> is thin orchestration inheriting never-clobber + --update. (b) Add net-new exportSubstrate(cwd,outDir): strip root + substrate_id, copy declared schemas, emit starter (or full) blocks, write a samples/-shaped conception.json. CRITICAL constraint: the 50KB output cap (DEFAULT_MAX_BYTES, truncate.ts:12; enforced in renderOpResultText + boundedJsonOutput, ops-registry.ts:127-199) means a whole-substrate bundle (100KB-1MB) CANNOT flow through the {json} channel — export MUST be a file-write op returning {read}/prose metadata (parity-check checkJsonContentCap would hard-fail {json:<content-read>}). import fits cleanly as a request->response write op (template amend-config, ops-registry.ts:1083). CLI exposure is automatic: useOps filters surface:'use' (cli.ts:44), zero CLI edits per op.

Fork 2 — NOT RESOLVABLE TODAY. resolveContextDir is unconditionally path.join(cwd, contextDir) (context-dir.ts:142) with no isAbsolute check and no ~ expansion; an absolute contextDir gets silently NESTED under cwd (path.join('/base','/abs')='/base/abs'), and bootstrap.schema.json puts no pattern on contextDir so it fails silently. Every path builder chains through that resolver (config/relations/schemas/migrations/agents/templates/blocks/object-store/locks/tmp-writes), so all inherit the breakage. listSubstrates only scans cwd by bare dir-name (index.ts:692,:709) so external dirs are invisible to switching. Enabling Fork 2 (no debt): isAbsolute branch in the resolver; a bootstrap-schema absolute-path policy; external-target handling in switchToExisting/switchAndCreate; an external-substrate discovery/registry; AND a substrate-level lock — the real gap: per-block locks (withBlockLock, block-api.ts:45) serialize on the absolute block path (cross-project-safe), but config.json/relations.json/migrations.json are written WITHOUT lock, so concurrent multi-project mutation races.

Identity crux (governs moving instance content): oid = sha256([substrate_id, nonce]) (mintOid, block-api.ts:624) is substrate-SCOPED — re-import yields a different oid; content_hash = sha256(content) (content-hash.ts:65) is GLOBALLY stable — enables cross-substrate dedup + integrity. So Fork 1 MODEL export is identity-clean; content export and Fork 2 sharing both need an oid-reconciliation policy (re-mint under target substrate_id, key on content_hash).

pi-workflows reality-check: 'import workflows' is actually directory-scan DISCOVERY (discoverWorkflows, workflow-discovery.ts — project .workflows/ > user ~/.pi/agent/workflows/ > bundled, dedup-by-name), NOT an import command or bundle transport. It is prior art for Fork 2's discovery angle, not Fork 1.

The forks are separable and need not land together. Fork 1 model export/import is lowest-risk (mostly generalization of existing machinery); Fork 2 touches the resolver everything chains through and needs the locking + identity work before it is safe.

dependencies: Node path.join POSIX semantics — path.join(cwd, absolutePath) nests rather than resets, which is why an absolute contextDir fails silently in resolveContextDir

stale_conditions: resolveContextDir (context-dir.ts) gains an isAbsolute / ~-expansion branch — Fork 2's primary blocker is removed and the 'not resolvable today' verdict no longer holds.
An export producer or /context import command lands — Fork 1's 'strictly one-directional / half-built' framing is superseded.
adoptConception / installContext change signature away from the hardcoded samples/ source assumed by the seam analysis.
The 50KB output cap (truncate.ts DEFAULT_MAX_BYTES) or the boundedJsonOutput / renderOpResultText boundary relocates or changes semantics — the 'export must be file-write' constraint may shift.
scripts/parity-check.ts changes its writer-classification / ctx-forwarding / {json}-content-cap gates — the parity obligations for new export/import ops change.
withBlockLock scope changes or a substrate-level lock is added — the Fork 2 cross-project concurrency gap is closed.
The oid/content_hash identity model (mintOid substrate-scoping; content_hash global stability) changes — the content-portability reconciliation requirement changes.

citations.label: ConfigBlock interface — model vs instance fields
REGISTRY_DESCRIPTORS — the portable registries
adoptConception — reads hardcoded samples/conception.json, never-clobber guard
installContext — copies installed_schemas/blocks from samples/ with skip-if-exists
initProject — bootstrap pointer + skeleton config + substrate_id
resolveContextDir — unconditional path.join(cwd, contextDir), no isAbsolute (Fork 2 blocker)
listSubstrates — cwd-bound bare-name discovery
withBlockLock — per-block lock; no substrate-global lock for config/relations/migrations
mintOid — substrate-scoped oid
computeContentHash — globally stable content hash
OpResult + 50KB cap at the output boundary
CLI reflection — useOps filters surface:'use'
parity-check gates — classification / ctx-forwarding / {json}-content-cap / dual-surface params
workflow-discovery — directory-scan, the real 'import workflows' prior art
full design doc (deep read)
